courses.description: The United Nation Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for 2015-30 30 were adopted to strive for a world that is ‘just, equitable and inclusive’. Those priority risk behaviours causing global burden of diseases are often established early in life and research findings have also confirmed the existence of common determinants (e.g., pathways in the brain) and the inter-relationships between the determinants of risk behaviours and behaviours themselves.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For TPg student in 
- Master of Public Health / Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health; - MSc/PgD in Epidemiology and Biostatistics; - MSc/PgD in Health Services Management; - MSc/PgD in Health Promotion and Behavioural Health programme.
courses.course_syllabus:
| **Topic** |
| --- |
| Intro to Health & Social Behavior Concepts/Course Overview [Before class, please watch the 11 mins video from US CDC “REACH: Improving Lives, Inspiring Hope” (<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6s6nhK4o_gU&list=PLvrp9iOILTQaklFMK28M7_0mju-qXnjjV&index=29>) |
| Tutorial 1: Quick review of basic concepts & overview of the Group Project (please download Group Project Proposal Form) |
| Theoretical Basis of Social & Behavioural Health Interventions I: (Health Belief Model and Theory of Reasoned Action/Planned Behavior) |
| Open period for projects discussion among group members |
| Theoretical Basis of Social & Behavioural Health Interventions II: (Social Cognitive Theory/Stages of Change/Social Support) |
| Open period for projects discussion among group members |
| Behavioural Health Interventions III: (Diffusion of Innovations) & Review of health behaviour theories |
| Free discussion time at end of review session to decide on target population & health behaviour intervention for group project |
| Health Promotion Needs Assessment & Objective Setting in the Planning phase of Health Promotion Programme Development |
| Health Promotion Programme Implementation & Process Evaluations with case study from Social Marketing |
| Health Promotion Project Implementation: Role of Government |
| Tutorial 2: Tutorial with project discussion of proper objective setting using S.M.A.R.T. (Submit Group Project Proposal Form to tutor at end of the tutorial session) |
| Midterm (covering lectures 1-6): MCQ & Essay format |
| Open group discussion session (Final approval of project topics given w/ comments by head tutor) |
| Health Promotion Project Implementation: Community Advocacy (Case study of Global Tobacco Control and Anti-smoking policies) |
| Tutorial 3: Programme Implementation Tutorial with project discussion |
| Health Promotion Programme Implementation: Healthy Settings Approach |
| Tutorial 4: Programme Implementation (part 2): 5 minutes presentation by each group on Project (limited to 4 slides) at end of tutorial session for feedback by tutors and classmates: Targeted population and health behaviour, rationale for project + primary outcome of interest+ main methods of intervention+ setting |
| Health Promotion Programme Monitoring & Evaluations |
| Tutorial 5: Monitoring and outcomes for project tutorial |
| Examples of health promotion in real life (online component) 1. Grass-roots school health promotion: UNICEF India: Teaching children how to improve health, hygiene, and sanitation (<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gHV6WC-TEwY>) 2. Discrimination & health promotion in resource poor communities: “China: Fighting HIV/AIDS” (<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-lW2V-6beAk>) . 3. Challenges of health promotion programmes facing political and religious opposition: “Pakistan conflict threatens polio campaign” (<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aV2F__PSyxk>) Please choose one of the above videos to do the 1 page brief exercise –download from Blackboard |
| Open discussion session for students to meet for project discussion |
| Tutorial 6: Group Project discussion with tutors and professors Turn in brief HW exercise from last week’s video |
| Final 20 minutes group presentation + 10 min Q & A session (Protocol due 2 weeks later by 11:59 PM) |
courses.required_readings:
* Glanz, K., Rimer, B.K., and Viswanath, K. 2008. *Health Behavior and Health Education: Theory, Research and Practice* (4th Edition). Jossey-Bass.
* Nutbeam, D., Harris, E., and Wise, M. 2010. *Theory in a nutshell: a guide to health promotion theory*. (3rd Edition) McGraw-Hill Book Company Australia.

Glanz, K. and Rimer, B.K.. 2005. Theory at a Glance: A Guide for Health Promotion Practice. US Natl Cancer Institute. http://www.sbccimplementationkits.org/demandrmnch/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/Theory-at-a-Glance-A-Guide-For-Health-Promotion-Practice.pdf

courses.subject: HPSB
courses.title: Health Promotion Programme Planning and Implementation
courses.description: This much needed course is designed to provide a practical course in the planning, needs assessment and development of health education and health promotion programmes.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For TPg student in
- Master of Public Health / Postgraduate Diploma in Public Health; - MSc/PgD in Epidemiology and Biostatistics; - MSc/PgD in Health Services Management; - MSc/PgD in Health Promotion and Behavioural Health programme.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Student should be able to
- Detail and apply the intervention mapping framework for the developing health promotion programmes
- To come up with concrete, logistically-feasible, theory-based health interventions
- Understand the components of health program planning and implementation
courses.course_syllabus:
| Health Promotion Planning: Two Perspectives |
| --- |
| Needs Assessment: What do they feel missing? |
| Segmentation: Who is the priority segment? |
| Exchange: What do they want in return? |
| Positioning: What is unique about our offer? |
| Marketing mix: How to deliver our offer? |
| Health Promotion Programme Management |
courses.required_readings: Kotler P, Lee NR. Social marketing: influencing behaviors for good. 4th edition. Thousand Oaks, CA: Sage. 2011.
